transcription: Yóò ràn ọ́ lọ́wọ́ pẹ̀lú ìrora àti ìrẹ̀wẹ̀sì rẹ.
translation: It will help you both with your pain and nausea.
reference: It will help you both with your pain and nausea.
hypothesis: Jo won lo pe awọn ara ati awọn iṣe.

transcription: Se oun kohun wa ti o le je ki Nkan yii fale . Mo ti rin lati KY si  VA sugbon mi o ti ri neurologist
translation: Is there anything that can slow this down .  I ve move from ky to va and have not found a neurologist .
reference: Is there anything that can slow this down .  I ve move from ky to va and have not found a neurologist .
hypothesis: She went to see a doctor at Ifale. She has been trying to get a KwI CV, but she cannot find a neurologist.

transcription: Lara awọn ti o pe tabi firanṣẹ awọn lẹta ni: Alakoso tẹlẹ, Gbogbogbo Ibrahim Babangida; Awọn Gomina ti Awọn odo, Nyesom Wike, Enugu, Ifeanyi Ugwuanyi, Yobe, Mai Mala Buni, Taraba, Darius Dickson Ishaku ati awọn ọmọ ile-iwe alakoso ti o forukọsilẹ ni 1953 ni Ile-iwe Aarin Katsina, nipasẹ olori wọn. Sen. Abba Ali.
translation: Among those who called or sent letters are: former President, General Ibrahim Babangida; Governors of Rivers, Nyesom Wike, Enugu, Ifeanyi Ugwuanyi, Yobe, Mai Mala Buni, Taraba, Darius Dickson Ishaku and the President’s classmates who enrolled in 1953 into the Katsina Middle School, through their leader Sen.  Abba Ali.
reference: Among those who called or sent letters are: former President, General Ibrahim Babangida; Governors of Rivers, Nyesom Wike, Enugu, Ifeanyi Ugwuanyi, Yobe, Mai Mala Buni, Taraba, Darius Dickson Ishaku and the President’s classmates who enrolled in 1953 into the Katsina Middle School, through their leader Sen.  Abba Ali.
hypothesis: There were those who came to France with a letter to Ibrahim Baba Ngida and the governors of Odo. They came to Enugu, Ifahin Igwahi. They also met Darius Dixon Isaku and the people of Alakoso who left since 1953 in the area of Katsina.
